title: Trigonometry, Vectors, and Periodic Systems · Decision Notebook: Vectors Components
summary: Plan bearings, slopes, cyclic demand, and resultant forces. Planned artifact: Add and verify the math int vectors components increment in a navigation and periodic-demand model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Resolve vectors into components and reconstruct magnitude and direction.
Choose laws of sines or cosines and detect ambiguous cases.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""applied-mathematics-intermediate cumulative model portfolio."""

from math import exp, log, sqrt


def reasonableness(value: float, lower: float, upper: float) -> bool:
    return lower <= value <= upper

steps.title: Calculate Vectors Components evidence
steps.instruction: Perpendicular components are 12 units and 16 units. Calculate the resultant magnitude. Use the result to decide Vectors Components for a navigation and periodic-demand model.
steps.why: The Vectors Components result for a navigation and periodic-demand model is incomplete until its unit, assumptions, scale, and tolerance survive a reasonableness check.

content.text: Use √(12² + 16²) and check that the result exceeds either component. Reconcile the result with this competency evidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Resolve vectors into components and reconstruct magnitude and direction.
